additionalTime: 10 minutes tomato and onion seasoning time plus 5 minutes sandwich pressing
author: Robert Carr
description: A cold Italian grinder sandwich with thin-sliced ham, Genoa salami, capicola, provolone, garlic-oregano mayo, seasoned tomatoes and onions, chopped giardiniera, pepperoncini, and a red wine vinaigrette grinder salad.

groceryIngredients.item: Italian sub roll or sturdy hoagie roll

groceryIngredients.display: paper-thin hot capicola or deli ham
iceberg lettuce or shredded iceberg lettuce

ingredients: 4 Italian sub rolls or sturdy hoagie rolls, 6 to 7 inches long, split but not fully separated
8 ounces paper-thin smoked deli ham
6 ounces Genoa salami, sliced paper-thin
6 ounces hot capicola or additional deli ham, sliced paper-thin
8 slices provolone cheese, about 6 ounces
4 cups finely shredded iceberg lettuce, from 1 small head or an 8-ounce bag
2 medium ripe tomatoes, sliced 1/4 inch thick
1/2 small red onion, very thinly sliced
1 (12-ounce) jar sliced pepperoncini peppers, using 1/2 cup peppers and 2 tablespoons pepperoncini brine
1 (12-ounce) jar mild or hot giardiniera, using 1/2 cup drained and finely chopped vegetables
1/2 cup mayonnaise
1 to 2 garlic cloves, finely grated or minced to a paste
1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
3 tablespoons red wine vinegar, divided
3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, divided
1 ounce Parmesan cheese, finely grated, about 1/2 cup loosely packed, divided
1 1/2 teaspoons dried oregano, divided
1 teaspoon garlic powder, divided
1/2 teaspoon onion powder
1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes, optional
1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 1/4 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
instructions: Place the 2 sliced tomatoes and the 1/2 thinly sliced red onion in a shallow bowl or on a plate.
Drizzle the tomatoes and onion with 1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil.
Season the tomatoes and onion with 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper.
Turn the tomatoes and onion gently with your hands or tongs until the oil, salt, and pepper coat both sides of the tomato slices.
Let the seasoned tomatoes and onion sit for 10 minutes while making the mayo and grinder salad.

Make the garlic-oregano mayo in a small bowl by stirring together 1/2 cup mayonnaise, 1 to 2 finely grated garlic cloves, 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard, 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar, 1 tablespoon pepperoncini brine, 2 tablespoons finely grated Parmesan cheese, 1/2 teaspoon dried oregano, 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper.
Taste the garlic-oregano mayo; it should be tangy, salty, garlicky, and slightly sharp from the vinegar and pepperoncini brine.
Refrigerate the garlic-oregano mayo while preparing the salad.
Drain 1/2 cup sliced pepperoncini peppers and 1/2 cup giardiniera well so the finished sandwiches stay crisp instead of watery.
Finely chop the 1/2 cup drained giardiniera into pieces about 1/4 inch wide.

Make the grinder salad dressing in a large bowl by whisking together the remaining 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar, remaining 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, remaining 1 tablespoon pepperoncini brine, remaining 1 teaspoon dried oregano, remaining 3/4 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon onion powder, 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes if using, remaining 1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon black pepper.
Add the 4 cups finely shredded iceberg lettuce, 1/2 cup drained sliced pepperoncini peppers, and 1/2 cup finely chopped giardiniera to the large bowl with the dressing.
Toss the grinder salad for 30 to 45 seconds, until every strand of lettuce is lightly coated and the pepperoncini and giardiniera are evenly distributed.
Open the 4 split Italian sub rolls or hoagie rolls without separating the hinges.
If the rolls are very thick, pinch out a shallow strip of bread from the inside of each top roll so the sandwiches close around the filling.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic-oregano mayo on the inside bottom half of each roll.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic-oregano mayo on the inside top half of each roll.
Place 1 slice provolone cheese on the bottom half of each mayo-coated roll.
Press 1 slice provolone cheese onto the inside top half of each mayo-coated roll so both sides of the bread have a cheese barrier.
Fold and layer 2 ounces paper-thin smoked deli ham over the bottom provolone on each sandwich.
Fold and layer 1 1/2 ounces paper-thin Genoa salami over the ham on each sandwich.
Fold and layer 1 1/2 ounces paper-thin hot capicola or additional deli ham over the salami on each sandwich.
Lift the seasoned tomato slices and red onion from their bowl, leaving extra tomato juices behind.
Place about 2 seasoned tomato slices and one-fourth of the seasoned red onion on each sandwich.
Sprinkle the remaining 6 tablespoons finely grated Parmesan cheese over the tomatoes and onion, using about 1 1/2 tablespoons Parmesan per sandwich.
Pile about 1 cup dressed grinder salad onto each sandwich.
Close each sandwich firmly so the dressed lettuce compresses into the meats and cheese.
Wrap each closed sandwich tightly in parchment paper or aluminum foil.
Press the wrapped sandwiches under a cutting board, sheet pan, or heavy skillet for 5 minutes so the rolls absorb the mayo and vinaigrette without falling apart.
Cut each cold Italian grinder sandwich in half through the wrapper, then unwrap and serve immediately.
notes: This is a no-cook sandwich, so the flavor comes from strong seasoning: garlic-oregano mayo, red wine vinegar, pepperoncini brine, chopped giardiniera, salty provolone, and seasoned tomatoes.
Ask the deli counter for the ham, salami, and capicola sliced paper-thin. Very thin slices fold into layers and are easier to bite through.
Use 1 garlic clove in the mayo if the cloves are very large, very fresh, or sharply spicy. Use 2 small to medium cloves when you want a stronger garlic bite.
Use hot capicola and hot giardiniera for a spicier sandwich, or use extra deli ham and mild giardiniera for a milder version.
Do not skip the 10-minute tomato and onion seasoning step. Cold raw tomatoes and onions need salt, pepper, and olive oil to taste finished inside a sandwich.
Drain the pepperoncini and giardiniera before adding them to the lettuce. Too much jar liquid makes the rolls soggy before the first bite.
Dress the iceberg lettuce right before assembly so it stays crisp but still picks up the oregano vinaigrette.
The recipe uses a restrained amount of salt because the deli meats, provolone, Parmesan, pepperoncini, and giardiniera already bring plenty of salt.
Use sturdy Italian sub rolls or hoagie rolls with a hinge. Very soft sandwich bread can tear once the grinder salad and seasoned tomatoes go in.
Splitting the provolone between the bottom and top of each roll helps protect the bread from the seasoned tomatoes, onion, and dressed grinder salad.
The 5-minute wrapped press helps the sandwich hold together and lets the mayo, vinaigrette, meat, cheese, tomato, onion, and salad combine.

These sandwiches are best right after pressing. For make-ahead, keep the garlic-oregano mayo, seasoned tomatoes and onions, and undressed lettuce mixture separate, then dress and assemble within 15 minutes of serving.
title: Cold Italian Grinder Sandwiches
